what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard breakdown item that describes a channels, members, invitations, and replies subsystem, splitting the content into SPECIFIED requirements, IMPLIED architecture, and proposed ADDITIONS.
observations: Title uses the BD-E breakdown prefix and covers channels, members, invitations, Claude-Code members, and replies.
Members can include Claude Code sessions, invited via codes, and must receive element IDs, addresses, context, envelopes, and annotated content.
A supervisor or coordinator is expected to route envelopes to members and handle auto-triage.
Replies are expected to flow back to Tim's phone overlay.
Proposes per-envelope assignment, per-envelope status visible to Tim, member presence, and structured decision questions from members to Tim.
Mentions ephemeral task-members that retire when done, noting a retirement component already exists.
References prerequisites such as three-channel convergence and the connector-client identity gap.
Has a part_of link to board://item-11da929d and is filed in the dragnet-development channel.
standouts: Claude Code sessions treated as channel members that can be invited and retired per task.
Requirement that members receive the full envelope, context, and annotated content to act.
Supervisor-mediated auto-triage with per-envelope status surfaced to Tim.
Structured decision/option questions from a member to Tim as a first-class interaction.
Ephemeral task-scoped membership with automatic retirement.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter, id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a part_of link to relate the item to a parent board item.
Content is organized into labeled narrative sections: SPECIFIED, IMPLIED, ADDITIONS.
Breaks strict separation of concerns by mixing access control, routing, UI, session lifecycle, and speculative architecture in one breakdown.
concerns: Several external concepts are referenced without explanation: Tim's phone overlay, three-channel convergence, and the connector-client identity gap.
The security and revocation model for invitations/codes is not described.
It assumes a retirement component already exists but gives no local detail.
The distinction between design members and code members is asserted but not defined.
Ambiguity about whether Claude Code members are automated agents, human operators, or spawned sessions.
notes: A design breakdown that expands channel membership into an access-and-routing model where Claude Code sessions can be invited as ephemeral members, receive full context, and send structured questions back to a human lead.
questions: What is the three-channel convergence mentioned as a prerequisite?
What is Tim's phone overlay and how does it receive replies?
How are invitations/codes generated, delivered, and revoked?
What exactly is included in the full envelope that members must receive?
How does the supervisor decide auto-triage assignments?
What is the connector-client identity gap?
Are ephemeral task-members scoped per envelope, per task, or per channel?
What is the decision/option schema for structured questions to Tim?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a breakdown of channels, members, invitations, Claude-Code members, and replies, though it also folds in routing, access, lifecycle, and human-in-the-loop additions.
apparent_themes: Membership as a routing and access primitive
intention_signals: It is trying to define a membership and routing layer so that channel annotations can be assigned to specialized members, including AI sessions, with bidirectional replies and human oversight, while preserving complete context.
novelty: Treating Claude Code sessions as inviteable, task-scoped channel members that retire after work, and allowing them to pose structured decision questions to a human lead.
contribution: It contributes the concept of extending channel membership to Claude Code/connector sessions, per-envelope assignment and status, full-envelope context delivery, auto-triage by a supervisor, and ephemeral task-members with structured human-in-the-loop questions.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard breakdown item defining a channels, members, invitations, and replies subsystem in which channel annotations are routed with full context to specialized members including Claude Code sessions, supervised by a coordinator, with per-envelope status, auto-triage, and ephemeral task-members.
intention_for_embedding: To specify the access, membership, routing, and reply mechanisms needed to assign channel work to human or Claude Code members and surface structured decisions back to the lead.
